title: Limited
subtitle: Limited series and miniseries that play like long films.

sections.name: I. High Priority
sections.desc: Strongest probable fits. Calibrated to your taste signal.

items.title: Edge of Darkness
items.whyPriority: Six BAFTAs. Sci-fi-adjacent in the same way Children of Men is — speculative thriller with weight. Best entry point on this tracker.
items.pitch: A police detective investigates his daughter's murder and uncovers a nuclear conspiracy. Genuinely extraordinary television.

critics.quote: Six BAFTAs including Best Drama Serial.
The Guardian, BFI, and Mark Lawson all rank it among the greatest TV dramas ever made.

items.title: Devs
items.whyPriority: Alex Garland on quantum determinism. Cold and structurally rigorous. The 'concept-with-character' balance you said you want.
items.pitch: A quantum-computing tech company believes it can model all of reality. Cold, deliberate.

critics.quote: Strongly positive. Praised the 'patient, accumulating dread.'

items.title: Counterpart
items.whyPriority: Cold-War espionage meets parallel-Earths. J.K. Simmons playing both selves. Smart structure, character-first. Cancelled but two seasons resolve.
items.pitch: A man split across two parallel Earths that diverged 30 years ago.

critics.quote: Highly positive. Called Simmons's dual performance 'a masterclass.'

items.title: Frank Herbert's Dune
items.whyPriority: Adapts the first novel more faithfully than Lynch's 1984 film. Theatrical production design. Won two Emmys.
items.pitch: John Harrison's adaptation. Slower, more political than Lynch's, with room to breathe across three nights.

critics.quote: Won Outstanding Cinematography and Outstanding Visual Effects for a Miniseries.
Now treated as the most narratively complete Dune adaptation prior to Villeneuve.

items.title: Frank Herbert's Children of Dune
items.whyPriority: Adapts both Dune Messiah AND Children of Dune. The only screen adaptation of either novel. Better than the 2000 series. Early James McAvoy.
items.pitch: Combines Dune Messiah and Children of Dune. Twelve years after Paul's ascension; the costs of empire come due.

critics.quote: The only viable screen treatment of the second and third Herbert novels.

sections.name: II. Medium Priority
sections.desc: Likely to land. Watch when high-priority queue exhausts.

items.title: The Day of the Triffids
items.whyPriority: BBC adaptation of Wyndham's 1951 novel. Pre-CGI practical effects. Three hours, manageable.
items.pitch: A meteor shower blinds most of humanity overnight, and the carnivorous, mobile triffid plants begin moving.

critics.quote: Mark Kermode and the British genre press treat it as the definitive adaptation.

items.title: The Leftovers
items.whyPriority: Sci-fi-adjacent. Two percent of population vanishes. S2 widely considered one of the best TV seasons ever. Bleakness in service of grace.
items.pitch: Damon Lindelof's redemption project after Lost. Bleak, but its bleakness is in service of grace.

critics.quote: Variety, NYT, Rolling Stone all rank it among the greatest TV series of the 2010s.

sections.name: III. Standard
sections.desc: Important entries with moderate confidence in fit.

items.title: The Prisoner
items.pitch: Patrick McGoohan as a former secret agent abducted to a surreal coastal village.

critics.quote: Foundational to all subsequent paranoid TV.

items.title: Quatermass and the Pit
items.pitch: Construction workers unearth what looks like a Nazi bomb in London, but it's far older — and Martian.

critics.quote: Foundational influence on Doctor Who, Alien, Carpenter's films.

items.title: Tales from the Loop
items.pitch: Anthology episodes set in a small town built atop an experimental physics facility. Adapted from Simon Stålenhag's paintings.

critics.quote: Mixed-positive. James Poniewozik admired the patience; some found it inert.

items.title: Threads
items.pitch: Documentary-style depiction of nuclear war's effect on Sheffield. The bleakest thing on either tracker.

critics.quote: Routinely cited as one of the most important pieces of television ever broadcast.

items.title: Black Mirror (selected episodes)
items.pitch: Watch only: 'The Entire History of You' (S1E3), 'Be Right Back' (S2E1), 'White Christmas' (special), 'San Junipero' (S3E4), 'USS Callister' (S4E1).

critics.quote: 'San Junipero' and 'USS Callister' both won Outstanding TV Movie.

sections.name: IV. Twin Peaks: The Return
sections.desc: A category of one. Demands prior knowledge of original Twin Peaks.

items.title: Twin Peaks: The Return
items.pitch: Lynch returned to Twin Peaks 25 years later and made what is essentially an 18-hour film. Episode 8 is the most extraordinary single hour of TV ever broadcast.

critics.quote: Named #1 film (not TV) of the year.
Sight & Sound's 2022 Greatest Films poll included it.
